mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising industrial production signals resilient supply-side fundamentals that offset rate hike headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when FEDFUNDS is rising, or direction flips to negative in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 260.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 1.0% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output, triggering immediate sell-offs in cyclical equities and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.
